IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PROJECT LEAD THE WAY (PLTW) EMPOWERS STUDENTS TO THRIVE IN AN EVOLVING WORLD.
IRS990.MissionDesc: PROJECT LEAD THE WAY (PLTW) EMPOWERS STUDENTS TO THRIVE IN AN EVOLVING WORLD.
IRS990.Desc: PLTW PROVIDES A TRANSFORMATIVE LEARNING EXPERIENCE FOR PREK-12 STUDENTS AND TEACHERS ACROSS THE U.S. THROUGH OUR PATHWAYS IN COMPUTER SCIENCE, ENGINEERING, AND BIOMEDICAL SCIENCE. PLTW EMPOWERS STUDENTS TO DEVELOP IN-DEMAND KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS NECESSARY TO THRIVE IN AN EVOLVING WORLD, AND PROVIDES TEACHERS WITH THE SUPPORT AND RESOURCES THEY NEED TO DEVOTE MORE TIME TO INSPIRING STUDENTS. PLTW STUDENTS AND TEACHERS FIND ADDITIONAL SUPPORT FROM OUR LARGER NETWORK OF PREK-12 SCHOOLS, ADMINISTRATORS COLLEGES AND UNIVERSITIES, AND CORPORATE AND PHILANTHROPIC PARTNER ACROSS THE COUNTRY UNITED AROUND A PASSION FOR PROVIDING STUDENTS WITH INSPIRING, ENGAGING, AND EMPOWERING LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES.

ContractorName.PersonNm: UNIV OF KANSAS CENTER FOR RESEARCH

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: $5,846 OF LOBBYING EXPENSES WERE MADE OF ALLOCATED EMPLOYEE TIME. $303,015 WAS MADE UP OF THIRD PARTY SERVICES.

ASC 740 DISCLOSURE: PLTW IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAX UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT OF UNRELATED BUSINESS TAXABLE INCOME AS DEFINED UNDER IRC SECTIONS 511 THROUGH 515 AND APPLICABLE SECTION UNDER THE INDIANA STATE TAX CODE, AND HAVE BEEN CLASSIFIED AS AN ORGANIZATION OTHER THAN A PRIVATE FOUNDATION. MANAGEMENT IS NOT AWARE OF ANY CIRCUMSTANCES THAT COULD IMPACT PLTW'S EXEMPT STATUS. PLTW HAD NO UNRECOGNIZED TAX BENEFITS OR UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS FOR THE YEARS ENDED JUNE 30, 2023 AND 2022, RESPECTIVELY. PLTW FILES EXEMPT ORGANIZATION RETURNS, AND, IF APPLICABLE, UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME TAX RETURNS IN THE U.S. FEDERAL, INDIANA AND CALIFORNIA JURISDICTIONS.
OTHER RECONCILING ITEMS: COST OF GOODS SOLD $ 53,170,733 LESS: DONATED SERVICES IN COGS $ (3,339,033) TOTAL $ 49,831,700
OTHER RECONCILING ITEMS: RETURN OF GRANTS RECEIVED IN PRIOR YEAR $35,000 PLEDGE DISCOUNTS $ 226,410 TOTAL $ 261,410
OTHER RECONCILING ITEMS: COST OF GOODS SOLD $ 53,170,733 ADD: RECOVERIES OF GRANTS PAID -$175,427 TOTAL $ 52,995,306

Hawaii District West Hawaii Complex Area

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCEDURES FOR MONITORING THE USE OF GRANTS: GRANT FUNDED SCHOOLS ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN APPLICATION OR THEY ARE RECOMMENDED FOR FUNDING BY PLTW STAFF. IF THEY ARE FUNDED THROUGH A COMPETITION, SCHOOLS SUBMIT APPLICATIONS WHICH ARE THEN REVIEWED AND SCORED BY INTERNAL REVIEWERS. IN THE INITIAL YEAR OF THE GRANT, ALL GRANT FUNDED SCHOOLS MUST SIGN A GRANT AGREEMENT. EACH SUBSEQUENT YEAR, PLTW REQUESTS SCHOOLS RECEIVING SUPPORT SUBMIT ANNUAL REPORTS THAT COLLECT INFORMATION ON STUDENT ENROLLMENT, ACCOMPLISHMENTS, CHALLENGES, MEDIA EXPOSURE, AND IN THE CASE OF CASH GRANTS, DETAILED INFORMATION ABOUT THEIR GRANT EXPENDITURES.
REASON FOR AMENDING: PART VII WAS AMENDED TO SHOW JAMES RAHN AS AN OFFICER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCESS TO REVIEW THE FORM 990: THE FORM 990 IS REVIEWED BY MEMBERS OF THE FINANCE TEAM, INCLUDING THE CFO. IT IS PROVIDED TO THE FULL VOTING BOARD FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO BEING SIGNED BY THE CFO AND THE FINAL VERSION IS PROVIDED TO THE FULL VOTING BOARD PRIOR TO FILING WITH THE IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCESS FOR MONITORING COMPLIANCE WITH CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY: COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY WAS MONITORED THROUGH ANNUAL STATEMENTS BEING DISTRIBUTED TO AND SIGNED BY MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS AND THE EXECUTIVE LEADERSHIP TEAM. IN ADDITION, THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY IS ACKNOWLEDGED AND SIGNED BY EACH EMPLOYEE UPON EMPLOYMENT. IF THERE IS A CONFLICT OF INTEREST, BOARD MEMBERS ABSTAIN FROM DISCUSSION AND VOTING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15A & 15B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: REVIEW OF CEO OR TOP MGMT OFFICIAL COMPENSATION: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS (THE CEO ABSTAINS FROM DISCUSSION AND VOTING) REVIEWS A WRITTEN COMPENSATION ANALYSIS RELATING TO THE EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION PACKAGE AND COMPETITIVE/COMPARABLE EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION BENCHMARKS WHICH MAY INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: (I) SALARY AND BENEFIT COMPENSATION STUDIES BY INDEPENDENT SOURCES, (II) WRITTEN JOB OFFERS FOR POSITIONS AT SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS, (III) DOCUMENTED TELEPHONE CALLS ABOUT SIMILAR POSITIONS, AND/OR (IV) INFORMATION OBTAINED FROM THE INTERAL REVENUE SERVICE FROM 990 FILINGS FOR SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS DOCUMENTS ITS DETERMINATIONS ON COMPENSATION FOR THE CEO BY RESOLUTION, WHICH INCLUDES THE EFFECTIVE DATE OF THE APPROVED COMPENSATION, THE MEMBERS OF THE BOARD WHO PARTICIPATED IN THE APPROVAL, REFERENCE TO THE COMPARABILITY/BENCHMARKING DATA AND ABSTENTIONS FROM VOTING ON COMPENSATION MATTERS DUE TO CONFLICT OF INTEREST. COMPENSATION WAS LAST REVIEWED IN JUNE 2023.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: OTHER ORGANIZATION DOCUMENTS PUBLICLY AVAILABLE: THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST AND THROUGH PUBLIC WEBSITES.
OTHER CHANGES IN NET ASSETS OR FUND BALANCES: RECOVERIES OF GRANTS PAID $175,427 PLEDGE DISCOUNTS -$226,410 TOTAL -$50,983

The related organizations had no activity during the year and remained dormant. Since dormant, we did not move forward in acquiring a 501(c)(3) determination letter for the not-for-profit organizations PLTW Foundation, Inc. and PLTW Holdings Inc.
